name: Tuscan Farro (Spelt) and White Bean Soup (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup chopped tomatoes, peeled and seeded
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over the beans and discard any stones.
2. Soak the beans overnight in a large bowl of water.
3. The next day: Place the farro in a bowl with plenty of water to cover. Let sit for 4 hours.
4. Drain the beans, place them in a saucepan and pour in enough water to cover by 2 inches. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer until tender, 45 to 60 minutes. Drain the beans and reserve the cooking liquid.
5. Puree about half of the beans with the cooking liquid to make a smooth paste. Set aside.
6. Warm the olive oil in a large soup pot over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, celery, rosemary, and garlic; cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are soft, 10 to 12 minutes.
7. Add the farro, whole beans, bean puree, tomatoes, and chicken stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer until the farro is tender and the soup is thick, 30 to 40 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.
8. Serve hot. If desired, drizzle with fruity extra virgin olive oil just before serving.
